review_reason: sorry remains in DensityFrameCondition.lean:181 - Case B (G(delta) in M for distinguishing formula) cannot be closed due to Lindenbaum GContent Control Problem. Case A is fully proven via double-density trick. See research-001 Finding 15 for analysis and recommended fallback (lexicographic product densification).

partial_progress.details: Phase 1-2 completed (Case A double-density trick fully proven). Phase 3 blocked: Case B branch of density_frame_condition has sorry. The Lindenbaum GContent Control Problem prevents controlling backward CanonicalR direction when G(delta) in M for all distinguishing formulas. Goal state in Case B: exists W, SetMaximalConsistent W AND CanonicalR M W AND CanonicalR W M' with hypotheses G(delta) in M, delta not in M, G(delta) in M', CanonicalR(M, M'), NOT CanonicalR(M', M).

completion_data.completion_summary: Proved density_frame_condition_caseA via novel double-density trick: when G(delta) not in M, construct two layers of witnesses and use temporal linearity to find intermediate. Case B (G(delta) in M for all distinguishing formulas) remains blocked with sorry due to Lindenbaum GContent Control Problem.

errors.message: sorry in density_frame_condition Case B branch at DensityFrameCondition.lean:181
errors.recommendation: Three options: (1) Show Case A formulas always exist (may be mathematically false under irreflexive semantics), (2) Use lexicographic product T_dense = StagedTimeline x_lex Q to bypass density proof (research-001 Finding 15), (3) Formalize selective Lindenbaum construction (Di Maio/Zanardo technique)
